description_long: This bird features a striking combination of deep red and blue plumage, with the red dominating the head, neck, and upper back, while the wings and tail are primarily blue with some black markings. The body is streamlined and elongated, with a long, tapering tail that extends well beyond the body. The wings are close to the body, and the feather pattern includes dark scalloped markings on the back and wings. The beak is short, curved, and pale in color, typical of seed-eating parrots. The legs are short and not prominently visible, likely gray or dark in color. The overall appearance is vibrant and distinctive, making it easily recognizable in urban parks or gardens.
description: A vibrant bird with deep red and blue plumage, a long tapering tail, and a short curved beak. Its body is streamlined with dark scalloped markings on the back and wings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated body, long blue tail, and red head with a curved beak.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the broad back, red and black feather pattern, and the full length of the blue tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same elongated form, red head, and blue-black wings and tail.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the red head, curved beak, and the symmetrical arrangement of the wings and tail.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Red head and underparts with blue wings and tail, black scalloped markings on back and wings
